notes: Default Kodak model for Tri-X 400. Combines the published E-31 table rows (1 sec → 2 sec, 10 sec → 50 sec, 100 sec → 1200 sec; Dev -10/-20/-30%) with points sampled from Kodak's adjusted-exposure graph between them (2/3/5/7/20/30/50/70 sec). Kodak lists no adjustment through 1/10 sec, so the no-correction band ends at 0.1 sec and corrected exposure interpolates in log-log space from the 0.1 sec knee through the anchors (the three published rows reproduce exactly; the intermediate points are graph estimates, not published table values). Inputs above 100 sec extrapolate the last segment and are flagged beyond the published source range.

source.title: KODAK PROFESSIONAL TRI-X 400 Film — Technical Data

profiles.name: Official Kodak table
notes: Source data sheet states no exposure adjustment is required from 1/1,000 sec to 1/10 sec.

Source data sheet also lists +1/3 stop at 1/10,000 sec for short-exposure (high-speed) photography; the long-exposure reciprocity table deliberately excludes that short-exposure anchor — it is preserved as a profile-level note only.

Kodak lists no exposure adjustment through 1/10 sec, so the no-correction band ends at 0.1 sec. At 1 sec Kodak's table gives +1/3 stop as an aperture/stop adjustment (no published corrected shutter time); the table uses the time-equivalent ≈1.26 sec (1 × 2^(1/3)) as a calculation anchor only — it is not presented as a Kodak-published corrected time. The 10 sec → 15 sec and 100 sec → 200 sec published corrected times reproduce exactly. Inputs above 100 sec extrapolate the last segment and are flagged beyond the published source range.

Kodak lists no exposure adjustment through 1/10 sec, so the no-correction band ends at 0.1 sec. At 1 sec Kodak's table gives +1/3 stop as an aperture/stop adjustment (no published corrected shutter time); the table uses the time-equivalent ≈1.26 sec (1 × 2^(1/3)) as a calculation anchor only — it is not presented as a Kodak-published corrected time. The 10 sec → 15 sec and 100 sec → 300 sec published corrected times reproduce exactly. Inputs above 100 sec extrapolate the last segment and are flagged beyond the published source range.

profiles.name: Official threshold and 120 sec filtration guidance

notes: No filter correction or exposure compensation required from 1/10,000 sec to 10 sec.

colorFilter.note: At 120 sec, add CC10R filtration per the published guidance.

note.text: Beyond 10 sec, follow the published 120 sec CC10R reference point; intermediate exposures are not quantified by the source.

source.title: KODAK PROFESSIONAL EKTACHROME E100 Film — Technical Data

profiles.name: Official constant correction across published 120–1000 s range

notes: Tc = √2 × Tm (constant +1/2 stop). Encodes Fujifilm's published 120–1000 sec range guidance verbatim. Above 1000 sec the same +1/2 stop continues as numeric continuation outside the published source range.

profiles.name: Official formula anchored to 1 s threshold

notes: Tc = Tm^p, p ≈ 1.1821. Threshold-anchored constrained log-log fit through Fujifilm's published 4/8/16/32 sec source references. 64 sec is not recommended.

profiles.name: Official formula anchored to 60 s threshold

notes: Tc = 60 × (Tm / 60)^p, p ≈ 1.2667. Threshold-anchored constrained log-log fit through the 60 s no-correction boundary and Fujifilm's published 2-min (+1/3 stop) and 4-min (+1/2 stop) references. Inputs above 240 sec are formula predictions outside the published source range.

profiles.name: Official formula anchored to 240 s reference

notes: Tc = 128 × (Tm / 128)^p, p ≈ 1.3676. Anchored to Fujifilm's published 4-min (240 s) +1/3-stop reference. 8 min is not recommended.

Official FOMA BOHEMIA multiplier rows at 1/10/100 sec (×2 / ×8 / ×16 → 2 / 80 / 1600 sec). Corrected exposure is interpolated between the published anchors in log-log space, so the anchors reproduce the data-sheet values exactly. Inputs above 100 sec extrapolate the last segment and are flagged beyond the published source range.

Official FOMA BOHEMIA multiplier rows at 1/10/100 sec (×3 / ×9 / ×18 → 3 / 90 / 1800 sec). Corrected exposure is interpolated between the published anchors in log-log space, so the anchors reproduce the data-sheet values exactly. Inputs above 100 sec extrapolate the last segment and are flagged beyond the published source range.

Official FOMA BOHEMIA multiplier rows at 1/10/100 sec (×1.5 / ×6 / ×8 → 1.5 / 60 / 800 sec). Corrected exposure is interpolated between the published anchors in log-log space, so the anchors reproduce the data-sheet values exactly. FOMA's published rows do not lie on a single power law, so the curve bends between the 10 sec and 100 sec anchors; the published rows stay visible as source evidence. Inputs above 100 sec extrapolate the last segment and are flagged beyond the published source range.

Official Rollei corrected-time rows (2 sec → 3 sec, 10 sec → 20 sec, 20 sec → 50 sec, 50 sec → 180 sec), interpolated between the published anchors in log-log space so the anchors reproduce the data-sheet values exactly. Rollei's published rows do not lie on a single power law, so the curve bends between anchors; the published rows stay visible as source evidence. Inputs above 50 sec extrapolate the last segment and are flagged beyond the published source range.

Official Rollei corrected-time rows (2 sec → 3 sec, 5 sec → 8 sec, 10 sec → 25 sec, 20 sec → 75 sec, 30 sec → 150 sec), interpolated between the published anchors in log-log space so the anchors reproduce the data-sheet values exactly. Rollei's published rows do not lie on a single power law, so the curve bends between anchors; the published rows stay visible as source evidence. Inputs above 30 sec extrapolate the last segment and are flagged beyond the published source range.

Official Rollei corrected-time rows (1 sec → 2 sec, 5 sec → 10 sec, 10 sec → 30 sec, 15 sec → 55 sec, 20 sec → 80 sec), interpolated between the published anchors in log-log space so the anchors reproduce the data-sheet values exactly. Rollei's published rows do not lie on a single power law, so the curve bends between anchors; the published rows stay visible as source evidence. Inputs above 20 sec extrapolate the last segment and are flagged beyond the published source range.

Official Rollei corrected-time rows (5 sec → 7.5 sec, 10 sec → 20 sec, 20 sec → 50 sec, 30 sec → 75 sec, 40 sec → 100 sec, 50 sec → 150 sec), interpolated between the published anchors in log-log space so the anchors reproduce the data-sheet values exactly. Rollei's published rows do not lie on a single power law, so the curve bends between anchors; the published rows stay visible as source evidence. Inputs above 50 sec extrapolate the last segment and are flagged beyond the published source range.

profiles.name: Official log-log formula fit through published quantified rows

notes: Tc = 0.9601 × Tm^p, p ≈ 1.5361. Free log-log fit through Rollei's four published quantified corrected-time rows (4/8/15/30 sec). The 1 sec and 2 sec published rows give corrected exposure as ranges (1–2 sec and 3–4 sec) and are preserved as source-evidence notes — they are never used as exact fitting points. Residuals at the four quantified rows stay within ~1/30 stop. Inputs above 30 sec continue on the same curve as numeric continuation outside the published source range.

note.text: Effective exposure 1 to 2 sec per published data sheet (range preserved as note).
Effective exposure 3 to 4 sec per published data sheet (range preserved as note).

profiles.name: Unofficial practical approximation
notes: Unofficial practical approximation from Stéphane Lafitte. Not Rollei-published reciprocity guidance.
Formula: Tc = Tm^1.62 for Tm > 1 sec. Lafitte context: long exposure with a B+W 10-stop ND filter.
Official Rollei product pages and PDFs identify the film stock but do not publish a Schwarzschild / reciprocity table for RETRO 400S.

Tc = Tm^1.62. Lafitte's published anchor examples (5 sec → 13.5 sec, 10 sec → 41 sec, 15 sec → 80 sec) define the practical source range through 15 sec; inputs above 15 sec continue on the same curve as numeric continuation outside the published source range.
5 sec → corrected 13.5 sec. Lafitte context: long exposure with a B+W 10-stop ND filter.
10 sec → corrected 41 sec. Lafitte context: long exposure with a B+W 10-stop ND filter.
15 sec → corrected 80 sec. Lafitte context: long exposure with a B+W 10-stop ND filter.

profiles.name: Official log-log formula fit through published quantified rows

notes: Tc = 0.9601 × Tm^p, p ≈ 1.5361. Free log-log fit through Rollei's four published quantified corrected-time rows (4/8/15/30 sec); identical shape to RETRO 80S because Rollei publishes the same correction values for both films. The 1 sec and 2 sec published rows give corrected exposure as ranges (1–2 sec and 3–4 sec) and are preserved as source-evidence notes — they are never used as exact fitting points. Residuals at the four quantified rows stay within ~1/30 stop. Inputs above 30 sec continue on the same curve as numeric continuation outside the published source range.

note.text: Effective exposure 1 to 2 sec per published data sheet (range preserved as note).
Effective exposure 3 to 4 sec per published data sheet (range preserved as note).

notes: Official ADOX corrected-time rows from the 2024 CHS 100 II technical sheet (2 sec → 3 sec, 4 sec → 8 sec, 8 sec → 20 sec, 15 sec → 45 sec), interpolated between the published anchors in log-log space so the anchors reproduce the data-sheet values exactly. The published multiplier rows stay visible as source evidence. The sheet does not publish reciprocity rows past 15 sec; inputs above 15 sec extrapolate the last segment and are flagged beyond the published source range.

source.citation: ADOX CHS 100 II technical sheet (11 July 2024)
source.title: ADOX CHS 100 II S/W Film — Technische Beschreibung, 11. Juli 2024

profiles.name: Official log-log formula anchored to 1 s and 10 s reference points

notes: Tc = 1.4142 × Tm^p, p ≈ 1.150515. Log-log fit through ADOX's published anchors at 1 sec (+1/2 stop) and 10 sec (+1 stop). Source-backed range ends at 10 sec; the 100 sec row is preserved as a 'Not recommended' warning marker (not a corrected-time anchor). Inputs above 10 sec still compute a formula-derived continuation but are classified outside source range; the user must read those values as formula prediction, not manufacturer guidance.
1/1000 sec → +1/2 stop. Preserved as published evidence only; the calculation path stays no-correction across the entire sub-1 sec band.
